item.title: Sanofi/Lunsekimig (SAR445229) Clinical Results
item.description:
• Summary: Sanofi's bispecific antibody Lunsekimig (SAR445229) achieved positive Phase 2 results in asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps, demonstrating significant reduction in symptoms and inflammation. However, it failed to meet endpoints in an atopic dermatitis (eczema) study, highlighting differential efficacy across allergic diseases. The drug targets key cytokines involved in type 2 inflammation.
  • MoAs: Lunsekimig is a bispecific antibody that simultaneously inhibits interleukin-4 (IL-4) and interleukin-13 (IL-13) signaling, modulating the type 2 immune response to reduce inflammation in allergic conditions.
  • Sources: BioSpace, Biopharma Dive, Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech
